channel.title: CyberNetSec - Phishing
channel.description: Latest phishing threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: 'FinReact' Phishing Campaign Uses AI-Generated Lures and 'GhostScript' Loader to Target Banks
item.description:
A highly sophisticated phishing campaign named 'FinReact' is targeting the financial services industry. Researchers at Proofpoint report that the campaign uses generative AI to create convincing, context-aware email lures that bypass traditional security. The attack chain involves a password-protect...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Microsoft Dismantles "StegoAd," a Malicious Edge Extension Campaign Using Steganography
item.description:
Microsoft has disrupted a major malicious browser extension campaign named 'StegoAd' that affected its Edge Add-ons store for over two years. The operation involved 119 extensions, downloaded by up to 2.6 million users, which used steganography to hide malicious JavaScript payloads within image and ...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Supply Chain Attack, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA
item.description:
Security researchers are warning about increasingly sophisticated email attacks, including the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. This adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attack uses a reverse proxy to present the victim with a real Microsoft login page. When the user authenticates and co...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: AssuranceAmerica Data Breach Impacts Over 1.1 Million, Exposing SSNs and Driver's Licenses
item.description:
AssuranceAmerica Managing General Agency, LLC has disclosed a major data breach affecting over 1.1 million individuals. The incident, which occurred in March 2026, stemmed from a targeted attack on an employee. The unauthorized access led to the theft of a vast amount of sensitive policyholder data,...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Over 200,000 Scam Sites Powered by Legitimate Chinese DCloud Framework, Infoblox Reports
item.description:
Cybersecurity firm Infoblox has uncovered a massive network of over 200,000 fraudulent websites being powered by a legitimate Chinese open-source framework called DCloud Uni-App. Threat actors are creating and selling scam templates built with the toolkit, enabling a wide array of investment scams, ...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Healthcare Tech Firm Xsolis Hit by Phishing Attack, Exposing Data of 1.4 Million People
item.description:
The healthcare technology firm Xsolis has reported a data breach affecting nearly 1.4 million individuals following a targeted phishing attack in January 2026. The incident resulted in unauthorized access to a vast amount of sensitive personal and protected health information (PHI), including Social...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Report: 60% of U.S. Manufacturers Hit by Email Breaches Amid Smart Factory Push
item.description:
A new report from Integris reveals that U.S. manufacturers are facing widespread cyber breaches as they accelerate their adoption of smart factory technologies. The survey found that a staggering 60% of manufacturing executives reported a significant email-based breach in the last year, and 49% expe...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Data Breach, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: Massive Malware Campaign Spreads via Compromised WhatsApp Accounts, Abusing User Trust
item.description:
Kaspersky's research team has uncovered a widespread malware campaign targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and Web. Attackers are using compromised WhatsApp accounts to send malicious VBScript files to the victims' contacts. The files are disguised as business documents to trick recipients into openi...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Malware, Phishing, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'OXLOADER' Malware Uses Malicious Google Ads to Distribute CastleStealer Infostealer
item.description:
A malvertising campaign (REF8372) is abusing Google Ads to distribute a new, sophisticated malware loader named OXLOADER. Users searching for legitimate software like 'node.js' are tricked into downloading a script from a fraudulent website. The script executes OXLOADER, which uses advanced evasion ...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Phishing, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'SquidLoader' Malware Uses Advanced Evasion to Target Chinese Orgs with Cobalt Strike
item.description:
A sophisticated and highly evasive malware loader, dubbed 'SquidLoader,' has been identified in phishing campaigns targeting organizations primarily in China since late April 2024. The malware is delivered via executable files disguised as Microsoft Word documents. SquidLoader employs a suite of adv...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Actor, Phishing

Read Full Article →
